ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Telur Bungkus Chicken; closest parent 'egg dish' (FOODON_03302051) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Egg dishes) is the closest parent category; no specific code exists for Telur Bungkus Chicken in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Telur Bungkus Chicken or a sufficiently close parent egg-and-chicken dish concept.

serving.description: 1 portion (1 egg wrap with chicken filling)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Telur Bungkus Chicken is approximately 180 g, comprising one whole egg omelette wrapped around a seasoned minced chicken filling, as typically served at a Malaysian or Singaporean hawker stall or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Egg omelette with minced meat (adapted); Chicken egg and minced chicken composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: IMR Malaysia MyFCD (Malaysian Food Composition Database) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Telur Bungkus Chicken exists in MyFCD or USDA. Values are adapted from MyFCD entry for 'Egg omelette with minced meat' (closest Malaysian composite dish) and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for whole egg (NDB 01123) and cooked ground chicken (NDB 05298). Nutrient values represent a weighted composite of approximately 55% egg omelette and 45% seasoned minced chicken filling per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of egg-based and mixed meat-egg dishes; analogous estimate from omelette with meat filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Telur Bungkus Chicken. GI estimated at 32 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for egg omelette with meat filling (GI ~30–35, low category). The dish is predominantly egg and minced chicken with minimal carbohydrate (2.8 g per 100 g), making a low GI classification highly defensible. Glycemic load per 180 g serving = (32 × 2.8 × 1.8) / 100 ≈ 1.6, confirming very low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~576 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful of the sodium contributed by soy sauce and seasoning in Telur Bungkus Chicken; a single 180 g serving provides approximately 576 mg sodium, representing roughly 25% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg, and portion control or requesting reduced-sodium preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (2.8 g per 100 g) and low glycemic index (~32)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Telur Bungkus Chicken is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index of approximately 32, resulting in a glycemic load of only 1.6 per standard serving, which produces minimal postprandial blood glucose elevation; it also provides high-quality protein that supports satiety and glycaemic stability.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol content (~245 mg per 100 g) and saturated fat (~3.6 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Telur Bungkus Chicken in moderation given its relatively high cholesterol content from whole egg (~441 mg per 180 g serving) and moderate saturated fat; however, the dish also provides beneficial monounsaturated fats and lean chicken protein, and occasional consumption as part of a balanced diet is generally acceptable when overall dietary cholesterol and saturated fat targets are managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Telur Bungkus Chicken (also known as Telur Bungkus Ayam or Chicken Wrapped Egg) is a popular Malaysian and Singaporean egg dish in which a thin, lightly seasoned egg omelette is wrapped around a filling of spiced minced chicken. The dish is prepared by first stir-frying minced chicken with aromatics such as onion, garlic, chilli, and soy sauce until cooked and fragrant, then encasing the filling within a pan-fried egg sheet that is folded or rolled into a parcel. The result is a golden-brown, slightly crispy exterior with a moist, savoury chicken interior. Texturally, the dish combines the soft, yielding quality of egg with the firmer, well-seasoned chicken mince. It is commonly served at Malaysian and Singaporean hawker centres, banana leaf restaurants, and mamak stalls, often accompanied by rice, roti, or flatbread. Nutritionally, Telur Bungkus Chicken is a high-protein, low-carbohydrate dish providing approximately 14.2 g protein and only 2.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making it particularly suitable for individuals managing blood glucose levels or following low-carbohydrate dietary patterns. Its low glycemic index (~32) and very low glycemic load (~1.6 per serving) make it a clinically favourable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish contains moderate sodium (~320 mg per 100 g) from soy sauce seasoning, which warrants attention for individuals with hypertension. Cholesterol content is relatively high due to the whole egg component, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume it in moderation. The dish is naturally halal and dairy-free, and contains egg and soy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Telur Bungkus?
Is Telur Bungkus good for diabetics?
What is chicken egg wrap in Malay?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Telur Bungkus Chicken exists in any approved primary source (MyFCD, HPB Foodo, USDA). Nutrient values are adapted from the closest Malaysian composite dish (egg omelette with minced meat) in MyFCD and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy raw ingredient data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD 'Egg omelette with minced meat' entry and USDA SR Legacy whole egg (NDB 01123) and cooked ground chicken (NDB 05298), weighted approximately 55:45 egg-to-chicken ratio per 100 g edible portion. No exact match exists in any approved primary source; source_match_type is 'adapted'. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on egg omelette with meat filling (GI range 30–35). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the adapted composite source and USDA SR Legacy; no sentinel -1 values are used as reasonable estimates are available from the adapted source. All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and reflect the egg-and-chicken composition of the dish. The dish is served at Singapore Banana Leaf Restaurant - Circular Road per field survey data (GD001223). Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules: English (food_name), Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are all provided. FoodOn and FoodEx2 use closest_parent match types as no exact ontology code exists for this specific dish. SNOMED CT match_type is none as no suitable concept was identified.
